reason: Response A shows the hallmarks of Opus-tier writing: more distinctive and varied phrasing ("a single moment, not a verdict," "this is a hard chapter, not the whole book"), a sustained metaphor, and a measured emotional intelligence that lingers on permission to grieve before pivoting to reframing. Response B covers the same beats competently but in flatter, more formulaic motivational-template language ("This is a setback, not an ending," "you have options"), which is more characteristic of a smaller model. The greater syntactic range and original imagery in A point to my own authorship.
